{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_fb6becac203b45b89ca64c99","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# ARMOR Error Testing Framework Guide\n2\t\n3\t## Overview\n4\t\n5\tThe ARMOR Error Testing Framework is a comprehensive, pattern-based testing infrastructure for validating S3-compatible error responses. This guide is designed for new developers to understand how to use, extend, and maintain the error testing system.\n6\t\n7\t## Table of Contents\n8\t\n9\t1. [Architecture Overview](#architecture-overview)\n10\t2. [Core Concepts](#core-concepts)\n11\t3. [Quick Start Guide](#quick-start-guide)\n12\t4. [Using Predefined Patterns](#using-predefined-patterns)\n13\t5. [Creating Custom Patterns](#creating-custom-patterns)\n14\t6. [Test Infrastructure](#test-infrastructure)\n15\t7. [Validation Helpers](#validation-helpers)\n16\t8. [Best Practices](#best-practices)\n17\t9. [Common Patterns](#common-patterns)\n18\t10. [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting)\n19\t\n20\t## Architecture Overview\n21\t\n22\tThe error testing framework is organized into several interconnected files:\n23\t\n24\t```\n25\tinternal/server/\n26\t├── error_test_patterns.go              # Core pattern definitions\n27\t├── error_test_infrastructure_test.go   # Test helpers and fixtures\n28\t├── error_test_patterns_base_test.go    # Test table structures\n29\t├── error_test_patterns_test.go         # Framework tests\n30\t├── http_error_fixtures.go              # HTTP response fixtures\n31\t└── error_pattern_usage_example.go      # Usage examples\n32\t```\n33\t\n34\t### File Responsibilities\n35\t\n36\t| File | Purpose |\n37\t|------|---------|\n38\t| `error_test_patterns.go` | Defines all error pattern structures, constants, and predefined patterns |\n39\t| `error_test_infrastructure_test.go` | Provides test server fixtures, validation helpers, and utility functions |\n40\t| `error_test_patterns_base_test.go` | Contains test table structures and predefined test tables |\n41\t| `error_test_patterns_test.go` | Tests for the framework itself |\n42\t| `http_error_fixtures.go` | HTTP error response fixtures for integration testing |\n43\t| `error_pattern_usage_example.go` | Executable examples demonstrating pattern usage |\n44\t\n45\t## Core Concepts\n46\t\n47\t### 1. Error Patterns\n48\t\n49\tError patterns are reusable configurations that define expected error responses. They encapsulate:\n50\t\n51\t- **HTTP Status Code**: The expected HTTP status (e.g., 404, 403)\n52\t- **S3 Error Code**: The S3-specific error code (e.g., \"NoSuchKey\")\n53\t- **Message Validation**: Expected message content and keywords\n54\t- **Performance Expectations**: Maximum acceptable response time\n55\t- **Category**: The error type (Auth, NotFound, InvalidRequest, etc.)\n56\t\n57\t### 2. Test Tables\n58\t\n59\tTest tables are collections of test cases that use error patterns. They enable:\n60\t\n61\t- **Table-Driven Testing**: Run multiple scenarios with consistent validation\n62\t- **Composability**: Combine base patterns with custom scenarios\n63\t- **Organization**: Group related tests by category or feature\n64\t\n65\t### 3. Validation Chain\n66\t\n67\tThe framework provides a fluent validation API for checking responses:\n68\t\n69\t```go\n70\tVerifyErrorResponse(t, response).\n71\t    HTTPStatusCode(404).\n72\t    HasCode(\"NoSuchKey\").\n73\t    MessageContainsAny(\"not\", \"found\").\n74\t    Assert()\n75\t```\n76\t\n77\t## Quick Start Guide\n78\t\n79\t### Step 1: Use a Predefined Pattern\n80\t\n81\tThe simplest way to start is using predefined patterns:\n82\t\n83\t```go\n84\timport \"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/server\"\n85\t\n86\tfunc TestMyFeatureErrors(t *testing.T) {\n87\t    fixture := server.NewTestServer(t)\n88\t    \n89\t    // Use the ResourceNotFound pattern\n90\t    pattern := server.CommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound\n91\t    \n92\t    // Make a request\n93\t    req := server.CreateTestRequest(t, \"GET\", \"/bucket/nonexistent\", nil, nil)\n94\t    duration, w := server.MeasureRequestTime(fixture.Handler, req)\n95\t    \n96\t    // Validate using pattern expectations\n97\t    server.VerifyErrorResponseWithTiming(t, w, duration).\n98\t        HTTPStatusCode(pattern.ExpectedStatus).\n99\t        HasCode(pattern.ExpectedCode).\n100\t        MessageContainsAny(pattern.ExpectedKeywords...).\n101\t        Assert()\n102\t}\n103\t```\n104\t\n105\t### Step 2: Run a Predefined Test Table\n106\t\n107\tFor comprehensive testing, use predefined test tables:\n108\t\n109\t```go\n110\tfunc TestAuthenticationErrors(t *testing.T) {\n111\t    fixture := server.NewTestServer(t)\n112\t    tests := server.StandardAuthenticationErrorTests()\n113\t    \n114\t    for _, tt := range tests {\n115\t        t.Run(tt.Name, func(t *testing.T) {\n116\t            server.RunAuthenticationTestCase(t, fixture, tt)\n117\t        })\n118\t    }\n119\t}\n120\t```\n121\t\n122\t### Step 3: Extend with Custom Tests\n123\t\n124\tAdd your custom tests to the base patterns:\n125\t\n126\t```go\n127\tfunc TestCustomScenarios(t *testing.T) {\n128\t    fixture := server.NewTestServer(t)\n129\t    \n130\t    // Start with base patterns\n131\t    baseTests := server.StandardAuthenticationErrorTests()\n132\t    \n133\t    // Add custom tests\n134\t    customTests := []server.AuthenticationErrorTestCase{\n135\t        {\n136\t            CommonErrorTestCase: server.CommonErrorTestCase{\n137\t                Name:        \"My custom auth scenario\",\n138\t                Description: \"Tests my specific authentication logic\",\n139\t                SetupRequest: func(t *testing.T) *http.Request {\n140\t                    return createMyCustomRequest(t)\n141\t                },\n142\t                ExpectedStatus: 403,\n143\t                ExpectedCode:   server.ErrorCodeAccessDenied,\n144\t            },\n145\t            AuthErrorType: \"CustomAuthFailure\",\n146\t        },\n147\t    }\n148\t    \n149\t    // Combine and run\n150\t    allTests := append(baseTests, customTests...)\n151\t    server.RunAuthenticationErrorTable(t, fixture, allTests)\n152\t}\n153\t```\n154\t\n155\t## Using Predefined Patterns\n156\t\n157\t### Common Error Patterns\n158\t\n159\tThe framework provides 8 common error patterns for everyday use:\n160\t\n161\t```go\n162\tserver.CommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound      // 404 - missing objects\n163\tserver.CommonErrorPatterns.AccessDenied          // 403 - access denied\n164\tserver.CommonErrorPatterns.InvalidRequest        // 400 - malformed requests\n165\tserver.CommonErrorPatterns.UnsupportedMediaType   // 415 - wrong content type\n166\tserver.CommonErrorPatterns.MethodNotAllowed      // 405 - unsupported HTTP method\n167\tserver.CommonErrorPatterns.InternalServerError    // 500 - server errors\n168\tserver.CommonErrorPatterns.SignatureMismatch     // 403 - signature validation\n169\tserver.CommonErrorPatterns.RequestExpired         // 403 - expired requests\n170\t```\n171\t\n172\t### Authentication-Specific Patterns\n173\t\n174\tFor detailed authentication testing, use auth-specific patterns:\n175\t\n176\t```go\n177\tserver.AuthErrorPatterns.MissingAuthHeader      // Missing Authorization header\n178\tserver.AuthErrorPatterns.InvalidAccessKeyId      // Invalid access key\n179\tserver.AuthErrorPatterns.SignatureDoesNotMatch   // Signature mismatch\n180\tserver.AuthErrorPatterns.MissingDateHeader       // Missing X-Amz-Date\n181\tserver.AuthErrorPatterns.RequestExpired          // Expired timestamp\n182\tserver.AuthErrorPatterns.MalformedAuthHeader     // Malformed auth header\n183\t```\n184\t\n185\t### Client vs Server Error Patterns\n186\t\n187\tOrganize tests by HTTP status categories:\n188\t\n189\t```go\n190\t// Client errors (4xx)\n191\tclientPatterns := []server.ErrorScenarioConfig{\n192\t    server.ClientErrorPatterns.BadRequest,\n193\t    server.ClientErrorPatterns.NotFound,\n194\t    server.ClientErrorPatterns.MethodNotAllowed,\n195\t    server.ClientErrorPatterns.UnsupportedMediaType,\n196\t}\n197\t\n198\t// Server errors (5xx)\n199\tserverPatterns := []server.ErrorScenarioConfig{\n200\t    server.ServerErrorPatterns.InternalError,\n201\t    server.ServerErrorPatterns.ServiceUnavailable,\n202\t}\n203\t```\n204\t\n205\t### Accessing Patterns by Category\n206\t\n207\tDynamically retrieve patterns by error category:\n208\t\n209\t```go\n210\t// Get all authentication error patterns\n211\tauthPatterns := server.PatternsForCategory(server.CategoryAuth)\n212\t\n213\t// Get all not-found patterns\n214\tnotFoundPatterns := server.PatternsForCategory(server.CategoryNotFound)\n215\t\n216\t// Iterate through patterns\n217\tfor _, pattern := range authPatterns {\n218\t    fmt.Printf(\"Pattern: %s (%d)\\n\", pattern.Name, pattern.ExpectedStatus)\n219\t}\n220\t```\n221\t\n222\t## Creating Custom Patterns\n223\t\n224\t### Option 1: Extend an Existing Pattern\n225\t\n226\tThe recommended approach is to start with a predefined pattern:\n227\t\n228\t```go\n229\tfunc createCustomNotFoundPattern() server.ErrorScenarioConfig {\n230\t    basePattern := server.CommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound\n231\t    \n232\t    return server.ErrorScenarioConfig{\n233\t        Name:              \"Custom Object Not Found\",\n234\t        ExpectedCode:      basePattern.ExpectedCode,\n235\t        ExpectedStatus:    basePattern.ExpectedStatus,\n236\t        ExpectedMessage:   \"Custom message: object not found\",\n237\t        ExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"custom\", \"not\", \"found\"},\n238\t        MinMessageLength:   basePattern.MinMessageLength,\n239\t        MaxResponseTime:   basePattern.MaxResponseTime,\n240\t        Description:       \"Custom not found scenario with specific messaging\",\n241\t        Category:          basePattern.Category,\n242\t    }\n243\t}\n244\t```\n245\t\n246\t### Option 2: Use the Pattern Builder\n247\t\n248\tFor programmatic pattern creation:\n249\t\n250\t```go\n251\tfunc createCustomAuthPattern() server.ErrorScenarioConfig {\n252\t    return server.ErrorScenarioConfig{\n253\t        Name:              \"Custom Auth Failure\",\n254\t        ExpectedCode:      \"CustomAuthError\",\n255\t        ExpectedStatus:    403,\n256\t        ExpectedMessage:   \"Custom authentication failed\",\n257\t        ExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"custom\", \"auth\", \"failed\"},\n258\t        MinMessageLength:  20,\n259\t        MaxResponseTime:   300 * time.Millisecond,\n260\t        Description:       \"Tests custom authentication scenario\",\n261\t        Category:          string(server.CategoryAuth),\n262\t    }\n263\t}\n264\t```\n265\t\n266\t### Option 3: Create from Scratch\n267\t\n268\tFor completely new error scenarios:\n269\t\n270\t```go\n271\tvar CustomErrorPatterns = struct {\n272\t    RateLimitExceeded server.ErrorScenarioConfig\n273\t    QuotaExceeded     server.ErrorScenarioConfig\n274\t}{\n275\t    RateLimitExceeded: server.ErrorScenarioConfig{\n276\t        Name:              \"Rate Limit Exceeded\",\n277\t        ExpectedCode:      \"RateLimitExceeded\",\n278\t        ExpectedStatus:    429,\n279\t        ExpectedMessage:   \"Rate limit exceeded\",\n280\t        ExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"rate\", \"limit\", \"exceeded\"},\n281\t        MinMessageLength:  20,\n282\t        MaxResponseTime:   100 * time.Millisecond,\n283\t        Description:       \"Tests rate limiting behavior\",\n284\t        Category:          \"RateLimit\",\n285\t    },\n286\t    QuotaExceeded: server.ErrorScenarioConfig{\n287\t        Name:              \"Quota Exceeded\",\n288\t        ExpectedCode:      \"QuotaExceeded\",\n289\t        ExpectedStatus:    429,\n290\t        ExpectedMessage:   \"Storage quota exceeded\",\n291\t        ExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"quota\", \"exceeded\"},\n292\t        MinMessageLength:  20,\n293\t        MaxResponseTime:   100 * time.Millisecond,\n294\t        Description:       \"Tests quota enforcement\",\n295\t        Category:          \"Quota\",\n296\t    },\n297\t}\n298\t```\n299\t\n300\t## Test Infrastructure\n301\t\n302\t### Test Server Fixture\n303\t\n304\tThe `TestServerFixture` provides a configured test server:\n305\t\n306\t```go\n307\tfixture := server.NewTestServer(t)\n308\t\n309\t// Access the HTTP handler\n310\thandler := fixture.Handler\n311\t\n312\t// Access the configuration\n313\tconfig := fixture.Config\n314\t```\n315\t\n316\t**Default Test Configuration:**\n317\t- Bucket: \"test-bucket\"\n318\t- Region: \"us-east-005\"\n319\t- Credentials: TESTACCESSKEY (full access), RESTRICTEDKEY (limited access)\n320\t\n321\t### Request Creation Helpers\n322\t\n323\tThe framework provides helpers for creating common test requests:\n324\t\n325\t```go\n326\t// Authentication failures\n327\tcreateMissingAuthHeaderRequest(t)\n328\tcreateInvalidKeyRequest(t)\n329\tcreateInvalidSignatureRequest(t)\n330\tcreateMalformedAuthRequest(t)\n331\tcreateMissingDateRequest(t)\n332\tcreateExpiredRequest(t)\n333\t\n334\t// Non-authentication failures\n335\tcreateNotFoundRequest(t)\n336\tcreateMethodNotAllowedRequest(t)\n337\tcreateUnsupportedMediaTypeRequest(t)\n338\tcreateMissingContentTypeRequest(t)\n339\t\n340\t// CORS requests\n341\tcreatePreflightRequest(t)\n342\t```\n343\t\n344\t**When to use each helper:**\n345\t\n346\t| Helper | When to Use |\n347\t|--------|-------------|\n348\t| `createMissingAuthHeaderRequest` | Testing missing Authorization header |\n349\t| `createInvalidKeyRequest` | Testing invalid access key ID |\n350\t| `createInvalidSignatureRequest` | Testing signature validation |\n351\t| `createMalformedAuthRequest` | Testing malformed auth header format |\n352\t| `createMissingDateRequest` | Testing missing X-Amz-Date header |\n353\t| `createExpiredRequest` | Testing timestamp validation |\n354\t| `createNotFoundRequest` | Testing 404 responses |\n355\t| `createMethodNotAllowedRequest` | Testing unsupported HTTP methods |\n356\t| `createUnsupportedMediaTypeRequest` | Testing content-type validation |\n357\t| `createPreflightRequest` | Testing CORS preflight |\n358\t\n359\t### Test Case Structures\n360\t\n361\tThe framework provides hierarchical test case structures:\n362\t\n363\t```go\n364\t// Base structure for all error tests\n365\ttype CommonErrorTestCase struct {\n366\t    Name                    string\n367\t    Description             string\n368\t    SetupRequest            func(*testing.T) *http.Request\n369\t    ExpectedStatus          int\n370\t    ExpectedCode            string\n371\t    ExpectedMessageKeywords []string\n372\t    MinMessageLength        int\n373\t    MaxResponseTime         time.Duration\n374\t    ValidateResponse        func(*testing.T, *httptest.ResponseRecorder)\n375\t}\n376\t\n377\t// Extended for authentication errors\n378\ttype AuthenticationErrorTestCase struct {\n379\t    CommonErrorTestCase\n380\t    AccessKey        string\n381\t    AuthErrorType    string\n382\t    ExpectedAuthError error\n383\t}\n384\t\n385\t// Extended for non-authentication errors\n386\ttype NonAuthenticationErrorTestCase struct {\n387\t    CommonErrorTestCase\n388\t    ErrorCategory string\n389\t    RequiresAuth  bool\n390\t    ResourcePath  string\n391\t}\n392\t\n393\t// Extended for CORS errors\n394\ttype CORSErrorTestCase struct {\n395\t    CommonErrorTestCase\n396\t    Origin             string\n397\t    ExpectedCORSOrigin string\n398\t    ExpectedCORSMethods string\n399\t    ExpectedCORSHeaders string\n400\t    IsPreflight        bool\n401\t}\n402\t```\n403\t\n404\t### Running Test Tables\n405\t\n406\tExecute entire test tables with consistent reporting:\n407\t\n408\t```go\n409\t// Authentication errors\n410\tserver.RunAuthenticationErrorTable(t, fixture, authTests)\n411\t\n412\t// Non-authentication errors\n413\tserver.RunNonAuthenticationErrorTable(t, fixture, nonAuthTests)\n414\t\n415\t// CORS errors\n416\tserver.RunCORSErrorTable(t, fixture, corsTests)\n417\t```\n418\t\n419\t## Validation Helpers\n420\t\n421\t### Fluent Validation API\n422\t\n423\tThe framework provides a chainable validation API:\n424\t\n425\t```go\n426\tserver.VerifyErrorResponse(t, response).\n427\t    HTTPStatusCode(404).\n428\t    ContentType(\"application/xml\").\n429\t    HasCode(\"NoSuchKey\").\n430\t    HasMessage(\"The specified key does not exist\").\n431\t    MessageContainsAny(\"not\", \"found\").\n432\t    MessageMinLength(15).\n433\t    ResponseTime(500 * time.Millisecond).\n434\t    HasCORSHeaders().\n435\t    CORSOrigin(\"*\").\n436\t    CORSMethods(\"GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS\").\n437\t    Assert()\n438\t```\n439\t\n440\t### Validation Method Reference\n441\t\n442\t| Method | Purpose | When to Use |\n443\t|--------|---------|-------------|\n444\t| `HTTPStatusCode(code)` | Validate HTTP status | Always required |\n445\t| `ContentType(type)` | Validate Content-Type header | Always required |\n446\t| `HasCode(code)` | Validate S3 error code | Always required |\n447\t| `HasMessage(msg)` | Validate exact message content | When exact message matters |\n448\t| `MessageContainsAny(keywords...)` | Validate message contains keywords | Prefer over exact message |\n449\t| `MessageMinLength(length)` | Validate minimum message length | Always required (default: 15) |\n450\t| `BodyNotEmpty()` | Ensure response has body | Always required |\n451\t| `HasXMLDeclaration()` | Ensure XML declaration present | Always required |\n452\t| `ResponseTime(duration)` | Validate response time | When performance matters |\n453\t| `HasCORSHeaders()` | Validate CORS headers present | For CORS scenarios |\n454\t| `CORSOrigin(origin)` | Validate CORS origin value | For CORS scenarios |\n455\t| `CORSMethods(methods)` | Validate CORS methods | For CORS scenarios |\n456\t| `CORSHeaders(headers)` | Validate CORS allowed headers | For CORS scenarios |\n457\t\n458\t### Timing-Aware Validation\n459\t\n460\tFor performance testing, use timing-aware validation:\n461\t\n462\t```go\n463\t// Measure request time\n464\tduration, w := server.MeasureRequestTime(fixture.Handler, request)\n465\t\n466\t// Validate with timing\n467\tserver.VerifyErrorResponseWithTiming(t, w, duration).\n468\t    HTTPStatusCode(404).\n469\t    ResponseTime(500 * time.Millisecond).  // Fails if exceeded\n470\t    Assert()\n471\t```\n472\t\n473\t### Custom Validation\n474\t\n475\tAdd custom validation logic:\n476\t\n477\t```go\n478\ttestCase := server.CommonErrorTestCase{\n479\t    Name: \"Custom validation example\",\n480\t    SetupRequest: func(t *testing.T) *http.Request {\n481\t        return createMyRequest(t)\n482\t    },\n483\t    ExpectedStatus: 404,\n484\t    ExpectedCode:   \"NoSuchKey\",\n485\t    ValidateResponse: func(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder) {\n486\t        // Custom validation beyond standard checks\n487\t        headers := w.Header()\n488\t        customHeader := headers.Get(\"X-Custom-Header\")\n489\t        if customHeader != \"expected-value\" {\n490\t            t.Errorf(\"Expected custom header 'expected-value', got '%s'\", customHeader)\n491\t        }\n492\t    },\n493\t}\n494\t\n495\tserver.RunCommonErrorValidations(t, w, testCase, duration)\n496\t```\n497\t\n498\t## Best Practices\n499\t\n500\t### 1. Use Predefined Patterns First\n501\t\n502\tAlways check if a predefined pattern fits your needs before creating custom ones:\n503\t\n504\t```go\n505\t// Good: Use predefined pattern\n506\tpattern := server.CommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound\n507\t\n508\t// Avoid: Reinventing the wheel\n509\tcustomPattern := server.ErrorScenarioConfig{\n510\t    ExpectedCode: \"NoSuchKey\",\n511\t    ExpectedStatus: 404,\n512\t    // ... redefining existing pattern\n513\t}\n514\t```\n515\t\n516\t### 2. Extend Rather Than Replace\n517\t\n518\tWhen you need customization, extend existing patterns:\n519\t\n520\t```go\n521\t// Good: Extend predefined pattern\n522\tbasePattern := server.CommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound\n523\tcustomPattern := basePattern\n524\tcustomPattern.ExpectedMessage = \"Custom message\"\n525\t\n526\t// Avoid: Complete rewrite\n527\tcustomPattern := server.ErrorScenarioConfig{\n528\t    // ... duplicating all base pattern fields\n529\t}\n530\t```\n531\t\n532\t### 3. Test Tables Over Individual Tests\n533\t\n534\tPrefer table-driven testing over individual test cases:\n535\t\n536\t```go\n537\t// Good: Table-driven test\n538\ttests := []server.AuthenticationErrorTestCase{\n539\t    { /* scenario 1 */ },\n540\t    { /* scenario 2 */ },\n541\t    { /* scenario 3 */ },\n542\t}\n543\tserver.RunAuthenticationErrorTable(t, fixture, tests)\n544\t\n545\t// Avoid: Separate test functions\n546\tfunc TestScenario1(t *testing.T) { /* ... */ }\n547\tfunc TestScenario2(t *testing.T) { /* ... */ }\n548\tfunc TestScenario3(t *testing.T) { /* ... */ }\n549\t```\n550\t\n551\t### 4. Descriptive Test Names\n552\t\n553\tUse clear, descriptive test names:\n554\t\n555\t```go\n556\t// Good: Descriptive name\n557\tserver.AuthenticationErrorTestCase{\n558\t    CommonErrorTestCase: server.CommonErrorTestCase{\n559\t        Name: \"Access denied with expired timestamp\",\n560\t        Description: \"Verify that requests with timestamps older than 15 minutes return 403 RequestExpired\",\n561\t        // ...\n562\t    },\n563\t}\n564\t\n565\t// Avoid: Vague name\n566\tserver.AuthenticationErrorTestCase{\n567\t    CommonErrorTestCase: server.CommonErrorTestCase{\n568\t        Name: \"Auth test\",\n569\t        // ...\n570\t    },\n571\t}\n572\t```\n573\t\n574\t### 5. Keyword-Based Message Validation\n575\t\n576\tPrefer keyword-based validation over exact message matching:\n577\t\n578\t```go\n579\t// Good: Flexible keyword matching\n580\tExpectedMessageKeywords: []string{\"not\", \"found\", \"exist\"}\n581\t\n582\t// Avoid: Brittle exact matching\n583\tExpectedMessageSubstring: \"The specified key does not exist\"\n584\t```\n585\t\n586\t### 6. Set Realistic Response Time Expectations\n587\t\n588\tBase response time expectations on your environment:\n589\t\n590\t```go\n591\t// Good: Environment-aware timing\n592\tMaxResponseTime: 500 * time.Millisecond,  // Adjusted for test environment\n593\t\n594\t// Avoid: Unrealistic expectations\n595\tMaxResponseTime: 1 * time.Millisecond,  // Too strict for most environments\n596\t```\n597\t\n598\t### 7. Use Helper Functions Appropriately\n599\t\n600\tMatch request creation helpers to your test scenario:\n601\t\n602\t```go\n603\t// Good: Use the right helper\n604\tcreateExpiredRequest(t)  // For timestamp validation\n605\t\n606\t// Avoid: Misusing helpers\n607\tcreateNotFoundRequest(t)  // Wrong for testing timestamp validation\n608\t```\n609\t\n610\t### 8. Validate Multiple Aspects\n611\t\n612\tDon't stop at status code validation:\n613\t\n614\t```go\n615\t// Good: Comprehensive validation\n616\tserver.VerifyErrorResponse(t, response).\n617\t    HTTPStatusCode(404).\n618\t    HasCode(\"NoSuchKey\").\n619\t    ContentType(\"application/xml\").\n620\t    MessageContainsAny(\"not\", \"found\").\n621\t    HasCORSHeaders().\n622\t    Assert()\n623\t\n624\t// Avoid: Minimal validation\n625\tif response.StatusCode != 404 {\n626\t    t.Errorf(\"Expected 404\")\n627\t}\n628\t```\n629\t\n630\t## Common Patterns\n631\t\n632\t### Pattern 1: Basic Error Validation\n633\t\n634\tThe most common pattern - validate a basic error response:\n635\t\n636\t```go\n637\tfunc TestBasicError(t *testing.T) {\n638\t    fixture := server.NewTestServer(t)\n639\t    \n640\t    req := server.CreateTestRequest(t, \"GET\", \"/bucket/missing\", nil, nil)\n641\t    w := httptest.NewRecorder()\n642\t    fixture.Handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n643\t    \n644\t    server.VerifyErrorResponse(t, w).\n645\t        HTTPStatusCode(404).\n646\t        HasCode(\"NoSuchKey\").\n647\t        ContentType(\"application/xml\").\n648\t        MessageMinLength(15).\n649\t        Assert()\n650\t}\n651\t```\n652\t\n653\t### Pattern 2: Pattern-Based Table Test\n654\t\n655\tUse predefined patterns for table-driven testing:\n656\t\n657\t```go\n658\tfunc TestPatternBasedTable(t *testing.T) {\n659\t    fixture := server.NewTestServer(t)\n660\t    \n661\t    scenarios := []struct {\n662\t        name    string\n663\t        pattern server.ErrorScenarioConfig\n664\t        request func(t *testing.T) *http.Request\n665\t    }{\n666\t        {\n667\t            name:    \"Not found\",\n668\t            pattern: server.CommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound,\n669\t            request: server.createNotFoundRequest,\n670\t        },\n671\t        {\n672\t            name:    \"Access denied\",\n673\t            pattern: server.CommonErrorPatterns.AccessDenied,\n674\t            request: server.createInvalidKeyRequest,\n675\t        },\n676\t    }\n677\t    \n678\t    for _, scenario := range scenarios {\n679\t        t.Run(scenario.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n680\t            req := scenario.request(t)\n681\t            duration, w := server.MeasureRequestTime(fixture.Handler, req)\n682\t            \n683\t            server.VerifyErrorResponseWithTiming(t, w, duration).\n684\t                HTTPStatusCode(scenario.pattern.ExpectedStatus).\n685\t                HasCode(scenario.pattern.ExpectedCode).\n686\t                MessageContainsAny(scenario.pattern.ExpectedKeywords...).\n687\t                ResponseTime(scenario.pattern.MaxResponseTime).\n688\t                Assert()\n689\t        })\n690\t    }\n691\t}\n692\t```\n693\t\n694\t### Pattern 3: Extending Standard Tables\n695\t\n696\tExtend standard test tables with custom scenarios:\n697\t\n698\t```go\n699\tfunc TestExtendedStandardTable(t *testing.T) {\n700\t    fixture := server.NewTestServer(t)\n701\t    \n702\t    // Get standard tests\n703\t    baseTests := server.StandardAuthenticationErrorTests()\n704\t    \n705\t    // Add custom tests\n706\t    customTests := []server.AuthenticationErrorTestCase{\n707\t        {\n708\t            CommonErrorTestCase: server.CommonErrorTestCase{\n709\t                Name:        \"Custom auth scenario\",\n710\t                Description: \"Test my custom authentication logic\",\n711\t                SetupRequest: func(t *testing.T) *http.Request {\n712\t                    return createMyCustomRequest(t)\n713\t                },\n714\t                ExpectedStatus: 403,\n715\t                ExpectedCode:   \"CustomAuthError\",\n716\t            },\n717\t            AuthErrorType: \"CustomAuthFailure\",\n718\t        },\n719\t    }\n720\t    \n721\t    // Combine and run\n722\t    allTests := append(baseTests, customTests...)\n723\t    server.RunAuthenticationErrorTable(t, fixture, allTests)\n724\t}\n725\t```\n726\t\n727\t### Pattern 4: Category-Based Testing\n728\t\n729\tTest all errors in a specific category:\n730\t\n731\t```go\n732\tfunc TestAllAuthErrors(t *testing.T) {\n733\t    fixture := server.NewTestServer(t)\n734\t    \n735\t    authPatterns := server.PatternsForCategory(server.CategoryAuth)\n736\t    \n737\t    for _, pattern := range authPatterns {\n738\t        t.Run(pattern.Name, func(t *testing.T) {\n739\t            req := createRequestForPattern(t, pattern)\n740\t            duration, w := server.MeasureRequestTime(fixture.Handler, req)\n741\t            \n742\t            server.VerifyErrorResponseWithTiming(t, w, duration).\n743\t                HTTPStatusCode(pattern.ExpectedStatus).\n744\t                HasCode(pattern.ExpectedCode).\n745\t                MessageContainsAny(pattern.ExpectedKeywords...).\n746\t                Assert()\n747\t        })\n748\t    }\n749\t}\n750\t```\n751\t\n752\t### Pattern 5: CORS Validation\n753\t\n754\tValidate CORS headers on error responses:\n755\t\n756\t```go\n757\tfunc TestCORSOnErrors(t *testing.T) {\n758\t    fixture := server.NewTestServer(t)\n759\t    \n760\t    tests := []server.CORSErrorTestCase{\n761\t        {\n762\t            CommonErrorTestCase: server.CommonErrorTestCase{\n763\t                Name:         \"404 with CORS\",\n764\t                SetupRequest: server.createNotFoundRequest,\n765\t                ExpectedStatus: 404,\n766\t                ExpectedCode:   \"NoSuchKey\",\n767\t            },\n768\t            Origin:             \"*\",\n769\t            ExpectedCORSOrigin: \"*\",\n770\t            ExpectedCORSMethods: \"GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS\",\n771\t        },\n772\t    }\n773\t    \n774\t    for _, tt := range tests {\n775\t        t.Run(tt.Name, func(t *testing.T) {\n776\t            req := tt.SetupRequest(t)\n777\t            req.Header.Set(\"Origin\", tt.Origin)\n778\t            \n779\t            duration, w := server.MeasureRequestTime(fixture.Handler, req)\n780\t            \n781\t            server.VerifyErrorResponseWithTiming(t, w, duration).\n782\t                HTTPStatusCode(tt.ExpectedStatus).\n783\t                HasCode(tt.ExpectedCode).\n784\t                CORSOrigin(tt.ExpectedCORSOrigin).\n785\t                CORSMethods(tt.ExpectedCORSMethods).\n786\t                Assert()\n787\t        })\n788\t    }\n789\t}\n790\t```\n791\t\n792\t## Troubleshooting\n793\t\n794\t### Common Issues and Solutions\n795\t\n796\t#### Issue 1: Test Fails with \"Expected status X, got Y\"\n797\t\n798\t**Problem:** HTTP status doesn't match expectation\n799\t\n800\t**Solutions:**\n801\t1. Verify the pattern's `ExpectedStatus` matches actual behavior\n802\t2. Check if request setup is correct (method, path, headers)\n803\t3. Ensure test server is properly configured\n804\t4. Verify authentication credentials are valid (if auth required)\n805\t\n806\t```go\n807\t// Debug: Print actual response\n808\tt.Logf(\"Actual status: %d\", w.Code)\n809\tt.Logf(\"Response body: %s\", w.Body.String())\n810\t```\n811\t\n812\t#### Issue 2: Test Fails with \"Expected code X, got Y\"\n813\t\n814\t**Problem:** S3 error code doesn't match expectation\n815\t\n816\t**Solutions:**\n817\t1. Verify error code is correctly defined in constants\n818\t2. Check if server is returning the expected error type\n819\t3. Ensure error code matches the actual failure scenario\n820\t4. Review error response XML to see actual code\n821\t\n822\t```go\n823\t// Debug: Parse and print error code\n824\tvar s3Err server.S3Error\n825\txml.Unmarshal(w.Body.Bytes(), &s3Err)\n826\tt.Logf(\"Actual error code: %s\", s3Err.Code)\n827\t```\n828\t\n829\t#### Issue 3: Test Fails with \"Message validation failed\"\n830\t\n831\t**Problem:** Message content doesn't match keywords\n832\t\n833\t**Solutions:**\n834\t1. Check if message keywords are too specific\n835\t2. Use `ExpectedMessageKeywords` instead of exact match\n836\t3. Verify message isn't changing between server versions\n837\t4. Consider relaxing validation if message format varies\n838\t\n839\t```go\n840\t// Debug: Print actual message\n841\tvar s3Err server.S3Error\n842\txml.Unmarshal(w.Body.Bytes(), &s3Err)\n843\tt.Logf(\"Actual message: %s\", s3Err.Message)\n844\t```\n845\t\n846\t#### Issue 4: Test Fails with \"Response time exceeded\"\n847\t\n848\t**Problem:** Response took longer than expected\n849\t\n850\t**Solutions:**\n851\t1. Adjust `MaxResponseTime` for your environment\n852\t2. Check if test environment is slower than expected\n853\t3. Verify no network delays or resource contention\n854\t4. Consider removing timing validation if not critical\n855\t\n856\t```go\n857\t// Debug: Print actual duration\n858\tt.Logf(\"Actual duration: %v\", duration)\n859\t```\n860\t\n861\t#### Issue 5: CORS Validation Fails\n862\t\n863\t**Problem:** CORS headers missing or incorrect\n864\t\n865\t**Solutions:**\n866\t1. Ensure `Origin` header is set on request\n867\t2. Verify CORS is enabled in server configuration\n868\t3. Check if CORS headers match expected values\n869\t4. Use `SkipCORSValidation: true` for non-CORS scenarios\n870\t\n871\t```go\n872\t// Debug: Print CORS headers\n873\tt.Logf(\"CORS Origin: %s\", w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\"))\n874\tt.Logf(\"CORS Methods: %s\", w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Methods\"))\n875\t```\n876\t\n877\t### Debug Mode\n878\t\n879\tEnable detailed logging for troubleshooting:\n880\t\n881\t```go\n882\tfunc TestWithDebugging(t *testing.T) {\n883\t    fixture := server.NewTestServer(t)\n884\t    \n885\t    req := server.CreateTestRequest(t, \"GET\", \"/bucket/key\", nil, nil)\n886\t    w := httptest.NewRecorder()\n887\t    fixture.Handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n888\t    \n889\t    // Debug output\n890\t    t.Logf(\"Request: %s %s\", req.Method, req.URL.Path)\n891\t    t.Logf(\"Status: %d\", w.Code)\n892\t    t.Logf(\"Headers: %v\", w.Header())\n893\t    t.Logf(\"Body: %s\", w.Body.String())\n894\t    \n895\t    // Then validate\n896\t    server.VerifyErrorResponse(t, w).\n897\t        HTTPStatusCode(404).\n898\t        Assert()\n899\t}\n900\t```\n901\t\n902\t### Getting Help\n903\t\n904\tIf you're stuck:\n905\t\n906\t1. **Check examples**: Review `error_pattern_usage_example.go`\n907\t2. **Read tests**: Look at `error_test_patterns_test.go`\n908\t3. **Verify patterns**: Run `error_patterns_verification_test.go`\n909\t4. **Consult docs**: Reference this guide and `error-responses.md`\n910\t\n911\t## Additional Resources\n912\t\n913\t### Code Examples\n914\t\n915\t- **Usage Examples**: `internal/server/error_pattern_usage_example.go`\n916\t- **Framework Tests**: `internal/server/error_test_patterns_test.go`\n917\t- **Verification Tests**: `internal/server/error_patterns_verification_test.go`\n918\t\n919\t### Documentation\n920\t\n921\t- **Error Reference**: `docs/error-responses.md`\n922\t- **Header Specification**: `docs/error-header-spec.md`\n923\t- **Response Inventory**: `docs/error-response-inventory.md`\n924\t\n925\t### Related Documentation\n926\t\n927\t- **S3 Error Codes**: [AWS S3 Error Response Reference](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/API/ErrorResponses.html)\n928\t- **HTTP Status Codes**: [MDN HTTP Status Reference](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Status)\n929\t- **CORS Specification**: [MDN CORS Guide](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/CORS)\n930\t\n931\t## Summary\n932\t\n933\tThe ARMOR Error Testing Framework provides:\n934\t\n935\t- **8 common error patterns** for everyday use\n936\t- **6 authentication-specific patterns** for auth testing\n937\t- **Fluent validation API** for response checking\n938\t- **Table-driven test infrastructure** for comprehensive testing\n939\t- **Extensible architecture** for custom scenarios\n940\t\n941\t### Key Takeaways\n942\t\n943\t1. **Start with predefined patterns** - Don't reinvent the wheel\n944\t2. **Use table-driven tests** - Better organization and reporting\n945\t3. **Validate comprehensively** - Check status, code, message, headers\n946\t4. **Extend, don't replace** - Build on existing patterns\n947\t5. **Be flexible with messages** - Use keywords, not exact matches\n948\t\n949\t### Quick Reference Card\n950\t\n951\t```go\n952\t// Create test server\n953\tfixture := server.NewTestServer(t)\n954\t\n955\t// Use predefined pattern\n956\tpattern := server.CommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound\n957\t\n958\t// Create request\n959\treq := server.CreateTestRequest(t, \"GET\", \"/bucket/key\", nil, nil)\n960\t\n961\t// Execute and measure\n962\tduration, w := server.MeasureRequestTime(fixture.Handler, req)\n963\t\n964\t// Validate response\n965\tserver.VerifyErrorResponseWithTiming(t, w, duration).\n966\t    HTTPStatusCode(pattern.ExpectedStatus).\n967\t    HasCode(pattern.ExpectedCode).\n968\t    MessageContainsAny(pattern.ExpectedKeywords...).\n969\t    ResponseTime(pattern.MaxResponseTime).\n970\t    Assert()\n971\t```\n972\t\n973\t---\n974\t\n975\t**Last Updated:** 2026-07-14  \n976\t**Framework Version:** 0.1.1741  \n977\t**Maintained By:** ARMOR Development Team\n978\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"f31a8090-4d15-42b5-bfb4-e1ae6789b8cb","uuid":"cdaca7da-ef44-4b96-8c29-e11f4730777d","timestamp":"2026-07-14T10:35:00.780Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/docs/error-testing-framework-guide.md","content":"# ARMOR Error Testing Framework Guide\n\n## Overview\n\nThe ARMOR Error Testing Framework is a comprehensive, pattern-based testing infrastructure for validating S3-compatible error responses. This guide is designed for new developers to understand how to use, extend, and maintain the error testing system.\n\n## Table of Contents\n\n1. [Architecture Overview](#architecture-overview)\n2. [Core Concepts](#core-concepts)\n3. [Quick Start Guide](#quick-start-guide)\n4. [Using Predefined Patterns](#using-predefined-patterns)\n5. [Creating Custom Patterns](#creating-custom-patterns)\n6. [Test Infrastructure](#test-infrastructure)\n7. [Validation Helpers](#validation-helpers)\n8. [Best Practices](#best-practices)\n9. [Common Patterns](#common-patterns)\n10. [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting)\n\n## Architecture Overview\n\nThe error testing framework is organized into several interconnected files:\n\n```\ninternal/server/\n├── error_test_patterns.go              # Core pattern definitions\n├── error_test_infrastructure_test.go   # Test helpers and fixtures\n├── error_test_patterns_base_test.go    # Test table structures\n├── error_test_patterns_test.go         # Framework tests\n├── http_error_fixtures.go              # HTTP response fixtures\n└── error_pattern_usage_example.go      # Usage examples\n```\n\n### File Responsibilities\n\n| File | Purpose |\n|------|---------|\n| `error_test_patterns.go` | Defines all error pattern structures, constants, and predefined patterns |\n| `error_test_infrastructure_test.go` | Provides test server fixtures, validation helpers, and utility functions |\n| `error_test_patterns_base_test.go` | Contains test table structures and predefined test tables |\n| `error_test_patterns_test.go` | Tests for the framework itself |\n| `http_error_fixtures.go` | HTTP error response fixtures for integration testing |\n| `error_pattern_usage_example.go` | Executable examples demonstrating pattern usage |\n\n## Core Concepts\n\n### 1. Error Patterns\n\nError patterns are reusable configurations that define expected error responses. They encapsulate:\n\n- **HTTP Status Code**: The expected HTTP status (e.g., 404, 403)\n- **S3 Error Code**: The S3-specific error code (e.g., \"NoSuchKey\")\n- **Message Validation**: Expected message content and keywords\n- **Performance Expectations**: Maximum acceptable response time\n- **Category**: The error type (Auth, NotFound, InvalidRequest, etc.)\n\n### 2. Test Tables\n\nTest tables are collections of test cases that use error patterns. They enable:\n\n- **Table-Driven Testing**: Run multiple scenarios with consistent validation\n- **Composability**: Combine base patterns with custom scenarios\n- **Organization**: Group related tests by category or feature\n\n### 3. Validation Chain\n\nThe framework provides a fluent validation API for checking responses:\n\n```go\nVerifyErrorResponse(t, response).\n    HTTPStatusCode(404).\n    HasCode(\"NoSuchKey\").\n    MessageContainsAny(\"not\", \"found\").\n    Assert()\n```\n\n## Quick Start Guide\n\n### Step 1: Use a Predefined Pattern\n\nThe simplest way to start is using predefined patterns:\n\n```go\nimport \"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/server\"\n\nfunc TestMyFeatureErrors(t *testing.T) {\n    fixture := server.NewTestServer(t)\n    \n    // Use the ResourceNotFound pattern\n    pattern := server.CommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound\n    \n    // Make a request\n    req := server.CreateTestRequest(t, \"GET\", \"/bucket/nonexistent\", nil, nil)\n    duration, w := server.MeasureRequestTime(fixture.Handler, req)\n    \n    // Validate using pattern expectations\n    server.VerifyErrorResponseWithTiming(t, w, duration).\n        HTTPStatusCode(pattern.ExpectedStatus).\n        HasCode(pattern.ExpectedCode).\n        MessageContainsAny(pattern.ExpectedKeywords...).\n        Assert()\n}\n```\n\n### Step 2: Run a Predefined Test Table\n\nFor comprehensive testing, use predefined test tables:\n\n```go\nfunc TestAuthenticationErrors(t *testing.T) {\n    fixture := server.NewTestServer(t)\n    tests := server.StandardAuthenticationErrorTests()\n    \n    for _, tt := range tests {\n        t.Run(tt.Name, func(t *testing.T) {\n            server.RunAuthenticationTestCase(t, fixture, tt)\n        })\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Step 3: Extend with Custom Tests\n\nAdd your custom tests to the base patterns:\n\n```go\nfunc TestCustomScenarios(t *testing.T) {\n    fixture := server.NewTestServer(t)\n    \n    // Start with base patterns\n    baseTests := server.StandardAuthenticationErrorTests()\n    \n    // Add custom tests\n    customTests := []server.AuthenticationErrorTestCase{\n        {\n            CommonErrorTestCase: server.CommonErrorTestCase{\n                Name:        \"My custom auth scenario\",\n                Description: \"Tests my specific authentication logic\",\n                SetupRequest: func(t *testing.T) *http.Request {\n                    return createMyCustomRequest(t)\n                },\n                ExpectedStatus: 403,\n                ExpectedCode:   server.ErrorCodeAccessDenied,\n            },\n            AuthErrorType: \"CustomAuthFailure\",\n        },\n    }\n    \n    // Combine and run\n    allTests := append(baseTests, customTests...)\n    server.RunAuthenticationErrorTable(t, fixture, allTests)\n}\n```\n\n## Using Predefined Patterns\n\n### Common Error Patterns\n\nThe framework provides 8 common error patterns for everyday use:\n\n```go\nserver.CommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound      // 404 - missing objects\nserver.CommonErrorPatterns.AccessDenied          // 403 - access denied\nserver.CommonErrorPatterns.InvalidRequest        // 400 - malformed requests\nserver.CommonErrorPatterns.UnsupportedMediaType   // 415 - wrong content type\nserver.CommonErrorPatterns.MethodNotAllowed      // 405 - unsupported HTTP method\nserver.CommonErrorPatterns.InternalServerError    // 500 - server errors\nserver.CommonErrorPatterns.SignatureMismatch     // 403 - signature validation\nserver.CommonErrorPatterns.RequestExpired         // 403 - expired requests\n```\n\n### Authentication-Specific Patterns\n\nFor detailed authentication testing, use auth-specific patterns:\n\n```go\nserver.AuthErrorPatterns.MissingAuthHeader      // Missing Authorization header\nserver.AuthErrorPatterns.InvalidAccessKeyId      // Invalid access key\nserver.AuthErrorPatterns.SignatureDoesNotMatch   // Signature mismatch\nserver.AuthErrorPatterns.MissingDateHeader       // Missing X-Amz-Date\nserver.AuthErrorPatterns.RequestExpired          // Expired timestamp\nserver.AuthErrorPatterns.MalformedAuthHeader     // Malformed auth header\n```\n\n### Client vs Server Error Patterns\n\nOrganize tests by HTTP status categories:\n\n```go\n// Client errors (4xx)\nclientPatterns := []server.ErrorScenarioConfig{\n    server.ClientErrorPatterns.BadRequest,\n    server.ClientErrorPatterns.NotFound,\n    server.ClientErrorPatterns.MethodNotAllowed,\n    server.ClientErrorPatterns.UnsupportedMediaType,\n}\n\n// Server errors (5xx)\nserverPatterns := []server.ErrorScenarioConfig{\n    server.ServerErrorPatterns.InternalError,\n    server.ServerErrorPatterns.ServiceUnavailable,\n}\n```\n\n### Accessing Patterns by Category\n\nDynamically retrieve patterns by error category:\n\n```go\n// Get all authentication error patterns\nauthPatterns := server.PatternsForCategory(server.CategoryAuth)\n\n// Get all not-found patterns\nnotFoundPatterns := server.PatternsForCategory(server.CategoryNotFound)\n\n// Iterate through patterns\nfor _, pattern := range authPatterns {\n    fmt.Printf(\"Pattern: %s (%d)\\n\", pattern.Name, pattern.ExpectedStatus)\n}\n```\n\n## Creating Custom Patterns\n\n### Option 1: Extend an Existing Pattern\n\nThe recommended approach is to start with a predefined pattern:\n\n```go\nfunc createCustomNotFoundPattern() server.ErrorScenarioConfig {\n    basePattern := server.CommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound\n    \n    return server.ErrorScenarioConfig{\n        Name:              \"Custom Object Not Found\",\n        ExpectedCode:      basePattern.ExpectedCode,\n        ExpectedStatus:    basePattern.ExpectedStatus,\n        ExpectedMessage:   \"Custom message: object not found\",\n        ExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"custom\", \"not\", \"found\"},\n        MinMessageLength:   basePattern.MinMessageLength,\n        MaxResponseTime:   basePattern.MaxResponseTime,\n        Description:       \"Custom not found scenario with specific messaging\",\n        Category:          basePattern.Category,\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Option 2: Use the Pattern Builder\n\nFor programmatic pattern creation:\n\n```go\nfunc createCustomAuthPattern() server.ErrorScenarioConfig {\n    return server.ErrorScenarioConfig{\n        Name:              \"Custom Auth Failure\",\n        ExpectedCode:      \"CustomAuthError\",\n        ExpectedStatus:    403,\n        ExpectedMessage:   \"Custom authentication failed\",\n        ExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"custom\", \"auth\", \"failed\"},\n        MinMessageLength:  20,\n        MaxResponseTime:   300 * time.Millisecond,\n        Description:       \"Tests custom authentication scenario\",\n        Category:          string(server.CategoryAuth),\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Option 3: Create from Scratch\n\nFor completely new error scenarios:\n\n```go\nvar CustomErrorPatterns = struct {\n    RateLimitExceeded server.ErrorScenarioConfig\n    QuotaExceeded     server.ErrorScenarioConfig\n}{\n    RateLimitExceeded: server.ErrorScenarioConfig{\n        Name:              \"Rate Limit Exceeded\",\n        ExpectedCode:      \"RateLimitExceeded\",\n        ExpectedStatus:    429,\n        ExpectedMessage:   \"Rate limit exceeded\",\n        ExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"rate\", \"limit\", \"exceeded\"},\n        MinMessageLength:  20,\n        MaxResponseTime:   100 * time.Millisecond,\n        Description:       \"Tests rate limiting behavior\",\n        Category:          \"RateLimit\",\n    },\n    QuotaExceeded: server.ErrorScenarioConfig{\n        Name:              \"Quota Exceeded\",\n        ExpectedCode:      \"QuotaExceeded\",\n        ExpectedStatus:    429,\n        ExpectedMessage:   \"Storage quota exceeded\",\n        ExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"quota\", \"exceeded\"},\n        MinMessageLength:  20,\n        MaxResponseTime:   100 * time.Millisecond,\n        Description:       \"Tests quota enforcement\",\n        Category:          \"Quota\",\n    },\n}\n```\n\n## Test Infrastructure\n\n### Test Server Fixture\n\nThe `TestServerFixture` provides a configured test server:\n\n```go\nfixture := server.NewTestServer(t)\n\n// Access the HTTP handler\nhandler := fixture.Handler\n\n// Access the configuration\nconfig := fixture.Config\n```\n\n**Default Test Configuration:**\n- Bucket: \"test-bucket\"\n- Region: \"us-east-005\"\n- Credentials: TESTACCESSKEY (full access), RESTRICTEDKEY (limited access)\n\n### Request Creation Helpers\n\nThe framework provides helpers for creating common test requests:\n\n```go\n// Authentication failures\ncreateMissingAuthHeaderRequest(t)\ncreateInvalidKeyRequest(t)\ncreateInvalidSignatureRequest(t)\ncreateMalformedAuthRequest(t)\ncreateMissingDateRequest(t)\ncreateExpiredRequest(t)\n\n// Non-authentication failures\ncreateNotFoundRequest(t)\ncreateMethodNotAllowedRequest(t)\ncreateUnsupportedMediaTypeRequest(t)\ncreateMissingContentTypeRequest(t)\n\n// CORS requests\ncreatePreflightRequest(t)\n```\n\n**When to use each helper:**\n\n| Helper | When to Use |\n|--------|-------------|\n| `createMissingAuthHeaderRequest` | Testing missing Authorization header |\n| `createInvalidKeyRequest` | Testing invalid access key ID |\n| `createInvalidSignatureRequest` | Testing signature validation |\n| `createMalformedAuthRequest` | Testing malformed auth header format |\n| `createMissingDateRequest` | Testing missing X-Amz-Date header |\n| `createExpiredRequest` | Testing timestamp validation |\n| `createNotFoundRequest` | Testing 404 responses |\n| `createMethodNotAllowedRequest` | Testing unsupported HTTP methods |\n| `createUnsupportedMediaTypeRequest` | Testing content-type validation |\n| `createPreflightRequest` | Testing CORS preflight |\n\n### Test Case Structures\n\nThe framework provides hierarchical test case structures:\n\n```go\n// Base structure for all error tests\ntype CommonErrorTestCase struct {\n    Name                    string\n    Description             string\n    SetupRequest            func(*testing.T) *http.Request\n    ExpectedStatus          int\n    ExpectedCode            string\n    ExpectedMessageKeywords []string\n    MinMessageLength        int\n    MaxResponseTime         time.Duration\n    ValidateResponse        func(*testing.T, *httptest.ResponseRecorder)\n}\n\n// Extended for authentication errors\ntype AuthenticationErrorTestCase struct {\n    CommonErrorTestCase\n    AccessKey        string\n    AuthErrorType    string\n    ExpectedAuthError error\n}\n\n// Extended for non-authentication errors\ntype NonAuthenticationErrorTestCase struct {\n    CommonErrorTestCase\n    ErrorCategory string\n    RequiresAuth  bool\n    ResourcePath  string\n}\n\n// Extended for CORS errors\ntype CORSErrorTestCase struct {\n    CommonErrorTestCase\n    Origin             string\n    ExpectedCORSOrigin string\n    ExpectedCORSMethods string\n    ExpectedCORSHeaders string\n    IsPreflight        bool\n}\n```\n\n### Running Test Tables\n\nExecute entire test tables with consistent reporting:\n\n```go\n// Authentication errors\nserver.RunAuthenticationErrorTable(t, fixture, authTests)\n\n// Non-authentication errors\nserver.RunNonAuthenticationErrorTable(t, fixture, nonAuthTests)\n\n// CORS errors\nserver.RunCORSErrorTable(t, fixture, corsTests)\n```\n\n## Validation Helpers\n\n### Fluent Validation API\n\nThe framework provides a chainable validation API:\n\n```go\nserver.VerifyErrorResponse(t, response).\n    HTTPStatusCode(404).\n    ContentType(\"application/xml\").\n    HasCode(\"NoSuchKey\").\n    HasMessage(\"The specified key does not exist\").\n    MessageContainsAny(\"not\", \"found\").\n    MessageMinLength(15).\n    ResponseTime(500 * time.Millisecond).\n    HasCORSHeaders().\n    CORSOrigin(\"*\").\n    CORSMethods(\"GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS\").\n    Assert()\n```\n\n### Validation Method Reference\n\n| Method | Purpose | When to Use |\n|--------|---------|-------------|\n| `HTTPStatusCode(code)` | Validate HTTP status | Always required |\n| `ContentType(type)` | Validate Content-Type header | Always required |\n| `HasCode(code)` | Validate S3 error code | Always required |\n| `HasMessage(msg)` | Validate exact message content | When exact message matters |\n| `MessageContainsAny(keywords...)` | Validate message contains keywords | Prefer over exact message |\n| `MessageMinLength(length)` | Validate minimum message length | Always required (default: 15) |\n| `BodyNotEmpty()` | Ensure response has body | Always required |\n| `HasXMLDeclaration()` | Ensure XML declaration present | Always required |\n| `ResponseTime(duration)` | Validate response time | When performance matters |\n| `HasCORSHeaders()` | Validate CORS headers present | For CORS scenarios |\n| `CORSOrigin(origin)` | Validate CORS origin value | For CORS scenarios |\n| `CORSMethods(methods)` | Validate CORS methods | For CORS scenarios |\n| `CORSHeaders(headers)` | Validate CORS allowed headers | For CORS scenarios |\n\n### Timing-Aware Validation\n\nFor performance testing, use timing-aware validation:\n\n```go\n// Measure request time\nduration, w := server.MeasureRequestTime(fixture.Handler, request)\n\n// Validate with timing\nserver.VerifyErrorResponseWithTiming(t, w, duration).\n    HTTPStatusCode(404).\n    ResponseTime(500 * time.Millisecond).  // Fails if exceeded\n    Assert()\n```\n\n### Custom Validation\n\nAdd custom validation logic:\n\n```go\ntestCase := server.CommonErrorTestCase{\n    Name: \"Custom validation example\",\n    SetupRequest: func(t *testing.T) *http.Request {\n        return createMyRequest(t)\n    },\n    ExpectedStatus: 404,\n    ExpectedCode:   \"NoSuchKey\",\n    ValidateResponse: func(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder) {\n        // Custom validation beyond standard checks\n        headers := w.Header()\n        customHeader := headers.Get(\"X-Custom-Header\")\n        if customHeader != \"expected-value\" {\n            t.Errorf(\"Expected custom header 'expected-value', got '%s'\", customHeader)\n        }\n    },\n}\n\nserver.RunCommonErrorValidations(t, w, testCase, duration)\n```\n\n## Best Practices\n\n### 1. Use Predefined Patterns First\n\nAlways check if a predefined pattern fits your needs before creating custom ones:\n\n```go\n// Good: Use predefined pattern\npattern := server.CommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound\n\n// Avoid: Reinventing the wheel\ncustomPattern := server.ErrorScenarioConfig{\n    ExpectedCode: \"NoSuchKey\",\n    ExpectedStatus: 404,\n    // ... redefining existing pattern\n}\n```\n\n### 2. Extend Rather Than Replace\n\nWhen you need customization, extend existing patterns:\n\n```go\n// Good: Extend predefined pattern\nbasePattern := server.CommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound\ncustomPattern := basePattern\ncustomPattern.ExpectedMessage = \"Custom message\"\n\n// Avoid: Complete rewrite\ncustomPattern := server.ErrorScenarioConfig{\n    // ... duplicating all base pattern fields\n}\n```\n\n### 3. Test Tables Over Individual Tests\n\nPrefer table-driven testing over individual test cases:\n\n```go\n// Good: Table-driven test\ntests := []server.AuthenticationErrorTestCase{\n    { /* scenario 1 */ },\n    { /* scenario 2 */ },\n    { /* scenario 3 */ },\n}\nserver.RunAuthenticationErrorTable(t, fixture, tests)\n\n// Avoid: Separate test functions\nfunc TestScenario1(t *testing.T) { /* ... */ }\nfunc TestScenario2(t *testing.T) { /* ... */ }\nfunc TestScenario3(t *testing.T) { /* ... */ }\n```\n\n### 4. Descriptive Test Names\n\nUse clear, descriptive test names:\n\n```go\n// Good: Descriptive name\nserver.AuthenticationErrorTestCase{\n    CommonErrorTestCase: server.CommonErrorTestCase{\n        Name: \"Access denied with expired timestamp\",\n        Description: \"Verify that requests with timestamps older than 15 minutes return 403 RequestExpired\",\n        // ...\n    },\n}\n\n// Avoid: Vague name\nserver.AuthenticationErrorTestCase{\n    CommonErrorTestCase: server.CommonErrorTestCase{\n        Name: \"Auth test\",\n        // ...\n    },\n}\n```\n\n### 5. Keyword-Based Message Validation\n\nPrefer keyword-based validation over exact message matching:\n\n```go\n// Good: Flexible keyword matching\nExpectedMessageKeywords: []string{\"not\", \"found\", \"exist\"}\n\n// Avoid: Brittle exact matching\nExpectedMessageSubstring: \"The specified key does not exist\"\n```\n\n### 6. Set Realistic Response Time Expectations\n\nBase response time expectations on your environment:\n\n```go\n// Good: Environment-aware timing\nMaxResponseTime: 500 * time.Millisecond,  // Adjusted for test environment\n\n// Avoid: Unrealistic expectations\nMaxResponseTime: 1 * time.Millisecond,  // Too strict for most environments\n```\n\n### 7. Use Helper Functions Appropriately\n\nMatch request creation helpers to your test scenario:\n\n```go\n// Good: Use the right helper\ncreateExpiredRequest(t)  // For timestamp validation\n\n// Avoid: Misusing helpers\ncreateNotFoundRequest(t)  // Wrong for testing timestamp validation\n```\n\n### 8. Validate Multiple Aspects\n\nDon't stop at status code validation:\n\n```go\n// Good: Comprehensive validation\nserver.VerifyErrorResponse(t, response).\n    HTTPStatusCode(404).\n    HasCode(\"NoSuchKey\").\n    ContentType(\"application/xml\").\n    MessageContainsAny(\"not\", \"found\").\n    HasCORSHeaders().\n    Assert()\n\n// Avoid: Minimal validation\nif response.StatusCode != 404 {\n    t.Errorf(\"Expected 404\")\n}\n```\n\n## Common Patterns\n\n### Pattern 1: Basic Error Validation\n\nThe most common pattern - validate a basic error response:\n\n```go\nfunc TestBasicError(t *testing.T) {\n    fixture := server.NewTestServer(t)\n    \n    req := server.CreateTestRequest(t, \"GET\", \"/bucket/missing\", nil, nil)\n    w := httptest.NewRecorder()\n    fixture.Handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n    \n    server.VerifyErrorResponse(t, w).\n        HTTPStatusCode(404).\n        HasCode(\"NoSuchKey\").\n        ContentType(\"application/xml\").\n        MessageMinLength(15).\n        Assert()\n}\n```\n\n### Pattern 2: Pattern-Based Table Test\n\nUse predefined patterns for table-driven testing:\n\n```go\nfunc TestPatternBasedTable(t *testing.T) {\n    fixture := server.NewTestServer(t)\n    \n    scenarios := []struct {\n        name    string\n        pattern server.ErrorScenarioConfig\n        request func(t *testing.T) *http.Request\n    }{\n        {\n            name:    \"Not found\",\n            pattern: server.CommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound,\n            request: server.createNotFoundRequest,\n        },\n        {\n            name:    \"Access denied\",\n            pattern: server.CommonErrorPatterns.AccessDenied,\n            request: server.createInvalidKeyRequest,\n        },\n    }\n    \n    for _, scenario := range scenarios {\n        t.Run(scenario.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n            req := scenario.request(t)\n            duration, w := server.MeasureRequestTime(fixture.Handler, req)\n            \n            server.VerifyErrorResponseWithTiming(t, w, duration).\n                HTTPStatusCode(scenario.pattern.ExpectedStatus).\n                HasCode(scenario.pattern.ExpectedCode).\n                MessageContainsAny(scenario.pattern.ExpectedKeywords...).\n                ResponseTime(scenario.pattern.MaxResponseTime).\n                Assert()\n        })\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Pattern 3: Extending Standard Tables\n\nExtend standard test tables with custom scenarios:\n\n```go\nfunc TestExtendedStandardTable(t *testing.T) {\n    fixture := server.NewTestServer(t)\n    \n    // Get standard tests\n    baseTests := server.StandardAuthenticationErrorTests()\n    \n    // Add custom tests\n    customTests := []server.AuthenticationErrorTestCase{\n        {\n            CommonErrorTestCase: server.CommonErrorTestCase{\n                Name:        \"Custom auth scenario\",\n                Description: \"Test my custom authentication logic\",\n                SetupRequest: func(t *testing.T) *http.Request {\n                    return createMyCustomRequest(t)\n                },\n                ExpectedStatus: 403,\n                ExpectedCode:   \"CustomAuthError\",\n            },\n            AuthErrorType: \"CustomAuthFailure\",\n        },\n    }\n    \n    // Combine and run\n    allTests := append(baseTests, customTests...)\n    server.RunAuthenticationErrorTable(t, fixture, allTests)\n}\n```\n\n### Pattern 4: Category-Based Testing\n\nTest all errors in a specific category:\n\n```go\nfunc TestAllAuthErrors(t *testing.T) {\n    fixture := server.NewTestServer(t)\n    \n    authPatterns := server.PatternsForCategory(server.CategoryAuth)\n    \n    for _, pattern := range authPatterns {\n        t.Run(pattern.Name, func(t *testing.T) {\n            req := createRequestForPattern(t, pattern)\n            duration, w := server.MeasureRequestTime(fixture.Handler, req)\n            \n            server.VerifyErrorResponseWithTiming(t, w, duration).\n                HTTPStatusCode(pattern.ExpectedStatus).\n                HasCode(pattern.ExpectedCode).\n                MessageContainsAny(pattern.ExpectedKeywords...).\n                Assert()\n        })\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Pattern 5: CORS Validation\n\nValidate CORS headers on error responses:\n\n```go\nfunc TestCORSOnErrors(t *testing.T) {\n    fixture := server.NewTestServer(t)\n    \n    tests := []server.CORSErrorTestCase{\n        {\n            CommonErrorTestCase: server.CommonErrorTestCase{\n                Name:         \"404 with CORS\",\n                SetupRequest: server.createNotFoundRequest,\n                ExpectedStatus: 404,\n                ExpectedCode:   \"NoSuchKey\",\n            },\n            Origin:             \"*\",\n            ExpectedCORSOrigin: \"*\",\n            ExpectedCORSMethods: \"GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS\",\n        },\n    }\n    \n    for _, tt := range tests {\n        t.Run(tt.Name, func(t *testing.T) {\n            req := tt.SetupRequest(t)\n            req.Header.Set(\"Origin\", tt.Origin)\n            \n            duration, w := server.MeasureRequestTime(fixture.Handler, req)\n            \n            server.VerifyErrorResponseWithTiming(t, w, duration).\n                HTTPStatusCode(tt.ExpectedStatus).\n                HasCode(tt.ExpectedCode).\n                CORSOrigin(tt.ExpectedCORSOrigin).\n                CORSMethods(tt.ExpectedCORSMethods).\n                Assert()\n        })\n    }\n}\n```\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n### Common Issues and Solutions\n\n#### Issue 1: Test Fails with \"Expected status X, got Y\"\n\n**Problem:** HTTP status doesn't match expectation\n\n**Solutions:**\n1. Verify the pattern's `ExpectedStatus` matches actual behavior\n2. Check if request setup is correct (method, path, headers)\n3. Ensure test server is properly configured\n4. Verify authentication credentials are valid (if auth required)\n\n```go\n// Debug: Print actual response\nt.Logf(\"Actual status: %d\", w.Code)\nt.Logf(\"Response body: %s\", w.Body.String())\n```\n\n#### Issue 2: Test Fails with \"Expected code X, got Y\"\n\n**Problem:** S3 error code doesn't match expectation\n\n**Solutions:**\n1. Verify error code is correctly defined in constants\n2. Check if server is returning the expected error type\n3. Ensure error code matches the actual failure scenario\n4. Review error response XML to see actual code\n\n```go\n// Debug: Parse and print error code\nvar s3Err server.S3Error\nxml.Unmarshal(w.Body.Bytes(), &s3Err)\nt.Logf(\"Actual error code: %s\", s3Err.Code)\n```\n\n#### Issue 3: Test Fails with \"Message validation failed\"\n\n**Problem:** Message content doesn't match keywords\n\n**Solutions:**\n1. Check if message keywords are too specific\n2. Use `ExpectedMessageKeywords` instead of exact match\n3. Verify message isn't changing between server versions\n4. Consider relaxing validation if message format varies\n\n```go\n// Debug: Print actual message\nvar s3Err server.S3Error\nxml.Unmarshal(w.Body.Bytes(), &s3Err)\nt.Logf(\"Actual message: %s\", s3Err.Message)\n```\n\n#### Issue 4: Test Fails with \"Response time exceeded\"\n\n**Problem:** Response took longer than expected\n\n**Solutions:**\n1. Adjust `MaxResponseTime` for your environment\n2. Check if test environment is slower than expected\n3. Verify no network delays or resource contention\n4. Consider removing timing validation if not critical\n\n```go\n// Debug: Print actual duration\nt.Logf(\"Actual duration: %v\", duration)\n```\n\n#### Issue 5: CORS Validation Fails\n\n**Problem:** CORS headers missing or incorrect\n\n**Solutions:**\n1. Ensure `Origin` header is set on request\n2. Verify CORS is enabled in server configuration\n3. Check if CORS headers match expected values\n4. Use `SkipCORSValidation: true` for non-CORS scenarios\n\n```go\n// Debug: Print CORS headers\nt.Logf(\"CORS Origin: %s\", w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\"))\nt.Logf(\"CORS Methods: %s\", w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Methods\"))\n```\n\n### Debug Mode\n\nEnable detailed logging for troubleshooting:\n\n```go\nfunc TestWithDebugging(t *testing.T) {\n    fixture := server.NewTestServer(t)\n    \n    req := server.CreateTestRequest(t, \"GET\", \"/bucket/key\", nil, nil)\n    w := httptest.NewRecorder()\n    fixture.Handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n    \n    // Debug output\n    t.Logf(\"Request: %s %s\", req.Method, req.URL.Path)\n    t.Logf(\"Status: %d\", w.Code)\n    t.Logf(\"Headers: %v\", w.Header())\n    t.Logf(\"Body: %s\", w.Body.String())\n    \n    // Then validate\n    server.VerifyErrorResponse(t, w).\n        HTTPStatusCode(404).\n        Assert()\n}\n```\n\n### Getting Help\n\nIf you're stuck:\n\n1. **Check examples**: Review `error_pattern_usage_example.go`\n2. **Read tests**: Look at `error_test_patterns_test.go`\n3. **Verify patterns**: Run `error_patterns_verification_test.go`\n4. **Consult docs**: Reference this guide and `error-responses.md`\n\n## Additional Resources\n\n### Code Examples\n\n- **Usage Examples**: `internal/server/error_pattern_usage_example.go`\n- **Framework Tests**: `internal/server/error_test_patterns_test.go`\n- **Verification Tests**: `internal/server/error_patterns_verification_test.go`\n\n### Documentation\n\n- **Error Reference**: `docs/error-responses.md`\n- **Header Specification**: `docs/error-header-spec.md`\n- **Response Inventory**: `docs/error-response-inventory.md`\n\n### Related Documentation\n\n- **S3 Error Codes**: [AWS S3 Error Response Reference](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/API/ErrorResponses.html)\n- **HTTP Status Codes**: [MDN HTTP Status Reference](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Status)\n- **CORS Specification**: [MDN CORS Guide](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/CORS)\n\n## Summary\n\nThe ARMOR Error Testing Framework provides:\n\n- **8 common error patterns** for everyday use\n- **6 authentication-specific patterns** for auth testing\n- **Fluent validation API** for response checking\n- **Table-driven test infrastructure** for comprehensive testing\n- **Extensible architecture** for custom scenarios\n\n### Key Takeaways\n\n1. **Start with predefined patterns** - Don't reinvent the wheel\n2. **Use table-driven tests** - Better organization and reporting\n3. **Validate comprehensively** - Check status, code, message, headers\n4. **Extend, don't replace** - Build on existing patterns\n5. **Be flexible with messages** - Use keywords, not exact matches\n\n### Quick Reference Card\n\n```go\n// Create test server\nfixture := server.NewTestServer(t)\n\n// Use predefined pattern\npattern := server.CommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound\n\n// Create request\nreq := server.CreateTestRequest(t, \"GET\", \"/bucket/key\", nil, nil)\n\n// Execute and measure\nduration, w := server.MeasureRequestTime(fixture.Handler, req)\n\n// Validate response\nserver.VerifyErrorResponseWithTiming(t, w, duration).\n    HTTPStatusCode(pattern.ExpectedStatus).\n    HasCode(pattern.ExpectedCode).\n    MessageContainsAny(pattern.ExpectedKeywords...).\n    ResponseTime(pattern.MaxResponseTime).\n    Assert()\n```\n\n---\n\n**Last Updated:** 2026-07-14
